name: Safe Wheels LLC
fleet_size_est: small van fleet (likely <15 vehicles; mix of large vans, mini vans, sedans)
districts_or_customers: Contracts with multiple southern New Hampshire school districts for special needs and McKinney-Vento transportation; also direct-to-family shuttle service
recent_news: Grand opening announced 2016 in Bedford, NH; family-owned operation focused on special needs/McKinney-Vento school transportation. No recent M&A activity identified.
fit_notes: Small NH-based family operator with niche special-needs/McKinney-Vento focus — a tuck-in fit for Summit's SPED-heavy strategy in New England, but very small scale (single-tier, ~$381K revenue, van fleet vs. Type C/D buses). Likely sub-scale on its own but could augment existing NH route density.

name: Midstate School Bus
owner_or_parent: unknown — address 1000 Race St, Plattsmouth now associated with First Student operations
fleet_size_est: unknown — likely very small (single-tier listing, 9 employees)
districts_or_customers: Likely Plattsmouth Community School District / Cass County area (unconfirmed)
recent_news: No direct M&A news for 'Midstate School Bus' identified. Note: distinct Mid States School Bus Inc. (Wayne, NE — ~200 buses, ~20 districts) was acquired by First Student in March 2021; the Plattsmouth 1000 Race St address is now a First Student depot, suggesting this entity may be defunct, a predecessor, or a small unrelated operator.
fit_notes: Potentially not an independent target — Plattsmouth address is now a First Student facility, indicating possible prior acquisition/absorption. Requires direct outreach/state corporate records check before classifying as a real tuck-in opportunity.

name: Old School Transport LLC
owner_or_parent: independent (per listing); Charles J Huften listed as owner
districts_or_customers: Likely small contract/subcontract work in NW Connecticut (Litchfield County area — Colebrook/Norfolk/Winsted/Northwestern Regional School District No. 7 vicinity); unconfirmed
recent_news: No M&A or news coverage identified. Owner Charles Huften also associated with New Beginnings Farm LLC at adjacent Colebrook address (2 Winsted Norfolk Rd), suggesting small lifestyle/family business.
fit_notes: Micro operator (5 employees, ~$349K revenue) in rural NW Connecticut; almost certainly sub-scale for direct tuck-in unless rolled up with adjacent small CT operators. Owner appears to run multiple small businesses — possible succession candidate but very small economic footprint.

name: Black Diamond Busing LLC
